task_completion.description: Create a new dashboard named 'Resource Correlation'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Resource Correlation' in the dashboard title or header area of the Graphite UI.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'CPU vs Disk Activity'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel within the dashboard that has the title 'CPU vs Disk Activity' displayed above it.

task_completion.description: Add the CPU utilization metric and set its alias to 'CPU Utilization %'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the metric 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' in the metric editor, and verify that the graph legend displays 'CPU Utilization %'.

task_completion.description: Add the disk write bytes metric and set its alias to 'Disk Write Bytes'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the metric 'servers.ec2_instance_1.disk.write_bytes' in the metric editor, and verify that the graph legend displays 'Disk Write Bytes'.

task_completion.description: Configure the graph to use a dual-axis by applying the secondYAxis function to the disk metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'secondYAxis' function applied to the disk metric in the editor, AND/OR look for a secondary Y-axis with numbers on the right side of the graph panel.
Look for a 'Dashboard saved' notification, a save dialog being confirmed, or the URL updating to reflect the saved dashboard name.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Graphite web UI to build the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Graphite web interface, adding metrics via the UI, rather than using terminal commands to inject a JSON file.

integrity.description: The graph renders actual data points for the metrics.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panel should display plotted lines or data points, indicating it successfully queried the real backend metrics, rather than being an empty graph or a mocked image.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by manually writing the dashboard JSON in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no screenshots of the agent using nano, vim, or another text editor to manually write the dashboard JSON structure.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'Resource Correlation'. The graph title must be 'CPU vs Disk Activity'. The primary Y-axis metric is 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' with alias 'CPU Utilization %'. The secondary Y-axis metric is 'servers.ec2_instance_1.disk.write_bytes' with alias 'Disk Write Bytes'. The dual-axis setup requires the 'secondYAxis' function applied specifically to the disk metric.
